So we're looking to increase the amount of time here history that we can provide to our customers through the platform.
And the second is the next level of iteration on what we're calling the visual benchmarking.
So really being able to see at a glance your position relative to your comps.

Um So before I open that up and share with you, um what would be the impact of being able to see historical rent data and have that um velocity of being able to iterate and, and uh see your position on a daily basis.
What, what, what, how would that change things for you compared to what you're using today?
Um I think for me, it's being able to see movement in the market.
Um There's some comps that for whatever reason, you know, we expected there to be a delta.

So we may not be pricing exactly in line but just trying to get a sense of general direction and even movement of other markets, some of the areas might draw traffic um from other regions, other areas.
And so say, you know, we're in a location that should be at a discount to another part of the, you know, city or area being able to see where those rents are trending as well up or down.
Um It's just helpful um for us to know where, where we should be.
So really, it's the trends um and some of the seasonal patterns as well, where were they priced at?
What part of their lease up?
How do they grow?
Um Is there a pattern within a certain time of the month or time of the year?
Um when rents go up or down?
So being able to see that kind of trajectory in the movement um both by some market and specific properties is helpful.
Yeah, I agree with that.
I think understanding any correlations between velocity pricing adjustments over obviously not sure but over longer periods of time is helpful for us to put together strategy.
And then I also think specifically within um areas where our subject property is the only lease up.
It is helpful to sort of zoom out of it and understand what the rent and velocity stories are in other areas.
And then we can adjust our marketing strategies um target other um submarkets in our paid search campaigns, et cetera.
So I think just understanding those trends when we're not directly competing or within the immediate area, um can just again um help us formulate a stronger, more cohesive strategy on the Lisa.

we, we, we used to use things like we used to use like radics back in the day.

we stabilized deals.

the cheapest asking rents and um

Do you want to speak to your markets?

Um
Yeah,

yeah, I mean, when you're looking at, when I look at the concept too, I always try to find, make sure that the available units or the, the leased units every month are at least a certain, you know, if everyone's leasing a percentage, like 25% of their, um, available units every week, then, you know, they're gonna fill their units by the end of the month.
Right.

If you see a lot of people that are under that, that rate, then you know, you're probably gonna see rates drop, right?

So um there's always that sort of business plan specific asset specific um strategy that, you know, could affect that sort of baseline that we start at the when we're budgeting for Lisa.

or would you?
